steps: Weight equals rate times volume: x = 160V.
Multiply all parts of the inequality by 160 (positive, so direction preserved): 160(12.7) < 160V < 160(15.7), i.e., 160(12.7) < x < 160(15.7).
choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '$160 - 15.7 < x < 160 - 12.7$': Uses subtraction instead of multiplication; weight doesn’t subtract from rate. ✗
  • Choice B — '$160 + 12.7 < x < 160 + 15.7$': Uses addition instead of multiplication; incorrect operation. ✗
  • Choice C — '$160(12.7) < x < 160(15.7)$': Multiplying the bounding volumes by 160 lb/ft³ gives the weight bounds directly. ✓
  • Choice D — '$\frac{160}{15.7} < x < \frac{160}{12.7}$': Division inverts the relationship and also reverses the bounds incorrectly. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
